reason: The accepted block sweep shows runtime is flat from 64 to 512 threads per block only at inner_iters=1024, which suggests block size may not matter in one compute-heavy regime. It is still unknown whether this invariance holds in the low-inner_iters regime, near the transition region, or at larger problem sizes. Measuring threads_per_block across multiple inner_iters can reveal occupancy, scheduling, or latency-hiding effects that are invisible at a single arithmetic intensity.
